Licensed Legal Paraprofessionals and Licensed Court-Access Assistants

Program Overview

The Texas Supreme Court issued preliminary approval of rules to create two new categories of legal paraprofessionals on August 6, 2024 (Misc. Docket No. 24-9050). The rules would allow qualified individuals to provide limited legal services directly to clients under the oversight of the State Bar of Texas.

Practice areas proposed under the rules include:

  • Family law
  • Estate planning and probate
  • Consumer debt
  • Justice court matters

Program Status

Current Status: Pending — Effective Date Delayed

On November 4, 2024, the Court issued an order (Misc. Docket No. 24-9095) delaying the effective date of the proposed rules in order to give due consideration to the comments received during the public comment period (open through November 1, 2024). No new effective date has been set.

No individuals have been licensed under this program. This page will be updated when the program becomes effective and the State Bar begins accepting applications.

Rulemaking Documents

  • Misc. Docket No. 24-9050 (August 6, 2024) — Preliminary Approval Order
  • Misc. Docket No. 24-9095 (November 4, 2024) — Delay Order
